codeReview.summary: Solid audit of 4 kernel files. One real bug found and fixed (ELF file_size > mem_size validation in load_segment) with proper TDD. All 10 unsafe blocks in address_space.rs verified with SAFETY comments. Break-before-make (Fix 10) re-verified sound — the sequence correctly zeros the entry, DSB ISH, TLBI, DSB ISH before writing the new valid descriptor. AddressSpace Drop (Fix 11) re-verified — catches partial allocation failures by calling invalidate_tlb() + free_all() + address_space_id::free(). Volatile upgrades to walk_or_create and map_inner are a valid code quality improvement (MMU may read concurrently). ASID wrapping/exhaustion verified correct. context.rs register save/restore verified against exception.S compile-time assertions. 5 well-targeted new tests cover the bug fix and edge cases. 387 tests pass, kernel builds.

issues.description: load_segment's offset computation (`ph_offset as usize + index * ph_ent_size`) and end computation (`offset + ph_ent_size`) use unchecked arithmetic. If ph_offset is very close to usize::MAX (e.g. u64::MAX - 10), the addition overflows — panicking in debug mode or wrapping in release mode. The worker noted that segment_data already uses checked_add (and tested that path), but did not apply the same discipline to load_segment's offset/end computation. In practice, the ELF data buffer is finite so a wrapped offset would likely fail the `end > data.len()` check in release mode, and the kernel trusts its own disk-loaded ELF data, so this is not a security issue. The test `load_segment_ph_offset_near_max` uses `u64::MAX - 100` which doesn't trigger the overflow (100 > 56), masking the edge case. Pre-existing issue (not introduced by this audit), but within audit scope since the feature description includes 'verify ELF header bounds checking'.

Inconsistent volatile discipline in free_all(): the L0/L1/L2 page table reads use plain dereferences (*l0_va.add(i0)) while walk_or_create and map_inner were upgraded to read_volatile. The free_all precondition requires invalidate_tlb() to have been called first, so the MMU shouldn't be reading these entries concurrently, making plain reads safe. But the inconsistency is worth noting for future maintainers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md convention says 'Prefer .checked_*() arithmetic over unchecked operations in kernel code', but load_segment's offset/end computation predates the audit and still uses unchecked addition. The worker audited this function and noted segment_data's checked_add but didn't flag/fix the inconsistency in load_segment. A more explicit note in AGENTS.md about applying checked arithmetic to ALL address/offset computations (not just the ones already using it) might help future auditors catch these.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/kernel/executable.rs:88 — `let offset = header.ph_offset as usize + (index as usize) * (header.ph_ent_size as usize);` uses unchecked add. Compare to system/kernel/executable.rs segment_data() which uses `start.checked_add(seg.file_size as usize)`.

summary: Pass. The audit is thorough and well-executed. One real bug (file_size > mem_size) found and fixed with proper TDD. All unsafe blocks verified with SAFETY comments. Break-before-make, AddressSpace Drop, ASID wrapping, ELF bounds, and register save/restore all verified. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) unchecked arithmetic in load_segment's offset computation (pre-existing, masked by test that doesn't quite reach the overflow threshold), and (2) inconsistent volatile discipline in free_all (safe due to precondition but inconsistent with the volatile upgrade applied elsewhere). Neither blocks acceptance — the audit meets all expectedBehavior criteria.
